artifacts.sourceTitle: Guiding Principles for the Use of Generative AI Tools
Developing Course AI Guidelines & Driving Class AI Discussion

artifacts.claimText: NC State guidance says several AI tools are available for use with university data at approved sensitivity levels, and use of university data with unapproved AI tools requires an IT Purchase Compliance request.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guiding Principles for the Use of Generative AI Tools
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Several AI Tools are now available for use with green (not sensitive), yellow (moderately sensitive) and red (highly sensitive) data campus-wide. Use of University Data with unapproved AI tools requires an IT Purchase Compliance (ITPC) request.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Principle 1, Ensure AI Tools are Approved Before Use

artifacts.claimText: NC State research AI guidance says researchers must handle data used with AI responsibly under applicable privacy laws, university policies, and NC State data management regulation, and AI use for research with specific compliance control plans needs approval before use.

citation.publisher: NC State University Artificial Intelligence Advisory Group
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Researchers must handle data used with AI research responsibly, adhering to all applicable privacy laws, university policies, and REG 08.00.03 - Data Management Regulation. ... For research that has specific compliance control plans, use of AI and the specific tools to be used need to be approved before use.

artifacts.title: North Carolina State University AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official NC State guidance on approved AI tools and university data, an approved AI solutions list, responsible AI use in research, and instructor-facing course AI guidance. Claims remain staged candidates and are not canonical public policy claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No login-gated or non-official sources were used.
The course AI guidance claim records the source's July 2025 no-official-policy statement and should be rechecked during human review because the broader AI guidance pages were updated in 2026.
